byKellyanne Stitts

COLUMBUS, Ohio (WSYX) —Columbus Crew champion goalie Patrick Schultereceived a life-changing phone callon Tuesday, July 2nd. The 23-year-old got the international call-up of his dreams, selected to represent the United States in the 2024 Paris Olympic Games.

"This is a dream come true for me," said Schulte. "It's going to be something that I'll remember forever."

Schulte's parents were in town, and the family shared the special moment that ended in tears for his mother.

Schulte will make the West Coast trip this weekend to face LAFC on Saturday at 10:30 ET before departing for France on Sunday. Schulte told our Kellyanne Stitts he is close with members on the U.S. Men's National Team and he has been corresponding with them to see what he needs to make for the road trip that they hope will end with gold. It's the first time the U.S. men's team has qualified for the Olympic Games since 2008.

Patrick Schulte one-on-one ahead of LAFC and Olympics

"We know the privilege that we have to represent our country," said Schulte. "We want to write our name in the history books, and we want to be the first men's team to come home with a medal. That's our focus. It's a true honor to be able to represent our country."

WSYX

The U.S.'s first match is against host France on Wednesday, July 24, two days before the Opening Ceremony.

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: 'My mom was crying,' Crew goalie Patrick Schulte talks about Olympic call-up
description: Columbus Crew champion goalie Patrick Schulte receieved a life-changing phone call on Tuesday, July 2nd.
